findings.summary: LIN bus interface not detected despite MCP2003B LIN transceiver being present on LINBUS net
findings.details: U4 is an MCP2003B LIN transceiver (lib keywords: 'LIN transceiver regulator') connected to the LINBUS net via its LBUS pin. The LINBUS net is also connected to the LIN filter (R6+C6, correctly detected as an RC low-pass) and the J4 interface connector. The analyzer's bus_analysis section has no 'lin' key and signal_analysis has no lin_interfaces detector. A LIN bus interface should be reported, recognizing U4 as the transceiver and LINBUS as the single-wire bus net.

findings.summary: Multi-rail power regulation chain (24V→12V→5V) correctly detected with proper topology
findings.details: The analyzer correctly identifies U1 (7812, L7812 LDO) converting +24V to +12V, and U2 (HT7550-1, matched to MCP1700x lib) converting +12V to +5V. Both appear in power_regulators with correct topology='LDO', input_rail, and output_rail fields. The SMF33A TVS on U4 (D3) is also correctly classified as a reverse_polarity protection device on the +24V rail.

findings.summary: DFM analysis correctly flags tight annular ring and assigns challenging tier
findings.details: The PCB has vias with 0.05 mm annular ring (drill 0.3 mm, pad 0.4 mm). The analyzer correctly identifies this as below the advanced process minimum of 0.1 mm, assigns dfm_tier='challenging', and reports one violation. Board dimensions (41.7 x 32.2 mm), 28 footprints, 183 track segments, 63 vias, and 2-layer stackup are all accurately reported.
